question: Rice ___ by farmers in many Asian countries.
explanation: Present Simple passivo: **is/are + particípio** — "Rice **is grown**". O "by farmers" mostra que a frase é passiva.

question: The Mona Lisa ___ by Leonardo da Vinci.
explanation: Passado + agente com "by" → Past Simple passivo: **was + particípio** ("was painted"). Sem o "to be" a frase fica errada.

question: These cookies ___ by my grandmother every Sunday.
explanation: Rotina no presente, sujeito recebe a ação → **are + made** (Present Simple passivo).

question: My phone ___ yesterday at the mall.
explanation: Passiva no passado: **was + particípio**. O particípio de steal é **stolen** ("stole" é o passado simples).

question: The office ___ by a professional team every evening.
explanation: Present Simple passivo: **is + cleaned**. "Is clean" (sem -ed) significa "está limpo" (adjetivo), não "é limpo (por alguém)".

question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The cake was ate by the kids.
The cake was eaten by the kids.
The cake eaten by the kids.
The cake was eat by the kids.
explanation: Passiva = to be + **particípio**: was + **eaten**. "Ate" é passado simples e não pode ser usado na passiva.

question: Spanish and English ___ in this school.
explanation: As línguas **recebem** a ação (alguém as ensina) → passiva: **are + taught**. O agente é irrelevante, por isso nem aparece.

question: The new hospital ___ by the mayor next month.
explanation: Futuro passivo: **will be + particípio** ("will be opened"). "Will be open" (sem -ed) é adjetivo = "estará aberto".

question: Transform into the passive: "They make these shoes in Italy."
options: These shoes are made in Italy.
These shoes are make in Italy.
These shoes is made in Italy.
explanation: Objeto vira sujeito + to be no mesmo tempo (Present Simple, plural → **are**) + particípio (**made**).

question: Transform: "Somebody has cleaned the room." → "The room ___."
explanation: Present Perfect passivo: **has/have + been + particípio** — "The room **has been cleaned**".

question: Many changes ___ since the new manager arrived.
explanation: Present Perfect passivo com sujeito plural ("changes") → **have been made**. "Has" seria para singular.

question: The thief ___ by the police last night.
explanation: Past Simple passivo: was + particípio. Catch é irregular: o particípio é **caught** ("catched" não existe).

question: Which sentence contains a mistake?
options: The book was wrote by a famous author.
The car was washed this morning.
explanation: Na passiva usamos o **particípio** (3ª coluna): "was **written**". "Wrote" é o passado simples.

question: Transform: "The company employs 200 people." → "200 people ___ by the company."
explanation: O objeto (200 people) vira sujeito plural → **are + employed** (Present Simple passivo).

question: Breakfast ___ from 7 to 10 every morning at this hotel.
explanation: O café da manhã não serve — ele **é servido** → **is served**. "Serves"/"is serving" fariam do breakfast o agente da ação.

question: Why is the passive voice used in "My wallet was stolen"?
options: We don't know who did the action
explanation: Usamos a passiva quando o **agente é desconhecido ou irrelevante** — não sabemos quem roubou, então o foco vai para a ação/objeto.

question: The invitations ___ to the guests yet.
explanation: Present Perfect passivo negativo: **haven't + been + particípio** — os convites não enviam, eles **são enviados**.

question: When ___ this castle built?
explanation: Pergunta na passiva do Past Simple: **was/were + sujeito + particípio** — "When **was** this castle built?". Com "did" seria voz ativa ("When did they build…?").

question: This medicine ___ before meals.
explanation: Passiva com modal: **modal + be + particípio** — "must **be taken**". O remédio não toma, ele **é tomado**.

question: Transform: "Scientists have discovered a new species." → "A new species ___ by scientists."
explanation: Present Perfect passivo com sujeito singular ("a new species") → **has been discovered**. "Have" seria para plural.

question: The report can't ___ until the data is checked.
explanation: Depois de modal (can't), a passiva usa **be + particípio**: "can't **be published**" — sem "to" e com o verbo no particípio.

question: By December, the new stadium ___ .
explanation: Future Perfect passivo: **will have been + particípio**. O estádio não completa nada — ele **será concluído**.

question: Choose the passive equivalent of: "They should have finished the work by Friday."
options: The work should have finished by Friday.
The work should be finished have by Friday.
The work should have been finished by Friday.
The work should have been finish by Friday.
explanation: Modal perfeito na passiva: **should have been + particípio** — "The work **should have been finished**".
